title: Neocinnamomum mekongense
taxonomy: Neocinnamomum mekongense is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae. It is a type of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Neocinnamomum mekongense is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Laurales. The plant is closely related to other members of the Cinnamomum genus, which is also part of the Lauraceae family. Neocinnamomum mekongense is a relatively newly discovered species, and further research is needed to fully understand its classification and characteristics.

intro: Neocinnamomum mekongense, also known as Thai cinnamon or Mekong cinnamon, is a plant species of the laurel family (Lauraceae) native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Neocinnamomum mekongense include cinnamaldehyde, eugenol, and limonene, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in aromatherapy and as a spice in culinary preparations.
